title: qualify cannot filter null value
description:
### What happens?

qualify cannot filter null value

### To Reproduce

```sql
CREATE TABLE test AS FROM (VALUES (1, 3), (3, 4)) t(col1, col2);
SELECT *, corr(1, 1) over () as a from test QUALIFY a IS NOT NULL;
```

the result as follows: 

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ col1  в”Ӯ col2  в”Ӯ   a    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ int32 в”Ӯ int32 в”Ӯ double в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ     1 в”Ӯ     3 в”Ӯ    nan в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ     3 в”Ӯ     4 в”Ӯ    nan в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test1 (col1 Int32, col2 Float32, col3 String);
INSERT INTO test1 VALUES (1, 3.5, 'A'), (2, NULL, 'B'), (3, 4.0, NULL);
SELECT *, corr(col1, col2) AS a FROM test1 HAVING a IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE test2 AS SELECT col1, col2 FROM test1 WHERE col3 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT col1, col2, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY col1) AS count_col1 FROM test2 HAVING count_col1 > 1;
